hypothesis: The computational cost of HMC refinement scales unfavorably with system size, but because the noise energy landscape ˆEθ learned by AMDEN implicitly encodes local structural relaxation pathways, a surrogate machine-learning interatomic potential trained directly on the gradient information generated during HMC denoising trajectories will reproduce the structural and energetic quality of HMC-refined amorphous structures at inference time without requiring iterative HMC steps, thereby reducing generation cost by at least an order of magnitude while maintaining property prediction accuracy.
